zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne Louise de Belleville於1300年1月15日出生在法國波代貝勒維爾-素-維時，她的父親Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu41歲，母親Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay25歲。她於大約1314年與Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1359年在法國布列塔尼莫比韓亨波特享年59歲，埋葬在法國布列塔尼o-度-諾爾德吉加普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne Louise de Belleville于1300年1月15日出生在法国波代貝勒維爾-素-維时，她的父亲Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu41岁，母亲Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay25岁。她于大约1314年与Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand在法国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1359年在法国布列塔尼莫比韓亨波特享年59岁，埋葬在法国布列塔尼o-度-諾爾德吉加普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Belleville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Carmelle, Gaston, Henri, Normand, Pierre.

French: habitational name from (La) Belleville or Belle Ville, names of several places in various parts of France, for example Belleville in Deux-Sèvres and Belleville-sur-Vie in Vendée, meaning literally ‘lovely settlement, domain’. Compare   Bellville  ,   Belveal  , and   Belville  .

History: One of the progenitors of the American Bellevilles was Jean (also Johannes) Belleville, a Frenchman who came from the Netherlands to Staten Island, NY, before 1670. His name is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. — In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jean Barthe/Barte dit Belleville and Larivière from France (see Barthe ).

variants.name: Belville
meanings: French: variant of   Belleville   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: This surname is listed along with its original form Belleville in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Bellville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Colette, Francois.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Louise
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Louis , introduced to England in the 17th century. Like Louisa , it has remained perennially popular and is currently frequently used as a component of compound names such as Ella-Louise, Sophie-Louise, and Tia-Louise.

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne Louise de Belleville was born on 15 January 1300, in Belleville-sur-Vie, Vendée, France, her father, Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu, was 41 and her mother, Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay, was 25. She married Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand about 1314, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1359, in Hennebont, Morbihan, Brittany, France, at the age of 59, and was buried in Guingamp, Côtes-du-Nord, Brittany, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne Louise de Belleville est née le 15 janvier 1300, à Belleville-sur-Vie, Vendée, France, son père, Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand environ 1314, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1359, à Hennebont, Morbihan, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Guingamp, Côtes-du-Nord, Bretagne, France.

de.Clisson, Loire-Atlantique, Pays de la Lo: Clisson, Loire-Atlantique, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich
de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne Louise de Belleville am 15. Januar 1300 in Belleville-sur-Vie, Vendée, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu 41 und ihre Mutter Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay 25. Sie hat um 1314 in Frankreich Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1359 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Hennebont, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich und wurde in Guingamp, Côtes-du-Nord, Bretagne, Frankreich begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne Louise de Bellevilleが1300年1月15日にフランスベンドeベレヴィレ-スル-ヴィで生まれた時，父Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaiguは41歳，母Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenayは25歳であった。約1314年にフランスでGeoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1359年にフランスブレタグンモービハンヘネボントで59歳で死去し，フランスブレタグンクoテス-ドウ-ノードグインガンップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne Louise de Belleville(이)가 1300년 1월 15일 프랑스 븐드e 벨레비레-수-비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1314년 프랑스에서 Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1359년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 헨본트에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 브레타그니 co츠-드-노르드 길그가프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne Louise de Belleville nasceu em 15 de janeiro de 1300, em Belleville-sur-Vie, Vendée, França, seu pai, Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand aproximadamente 1314, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1359, em Hennebont, Morbihan, Bretagne, França, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em Guingamp, Côtes-du-Nord, Bretagne, França.

ru.Clisson, Loire-Atlantique, Pays de la Lo: клиссон, лоире-атлантику, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne Louise de Belleville родилась 15 января 1300 г. в местности беллевилле-сур-ви, вендe, Франция, её отцу по имени Maurice IV de Belleville-Montaigu было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Létice Isabelle l'Archevêque de Parthenay, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand около 1314 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1359 г. в местности хеннебонт, морбихань, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности гуингамп, кoтес-ду-норд, бретагн, Франция.

Se casó con Geoffroy VIII de Châteaubriand, Seigneur de Châteaubriand aproximadamente 1314, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
